lessons.title: What recovery means: defining your own path
lessons.description: An exploratory session helping participants articulate their personal definition of recovery, moving beyond abstinence to encompass health, relationships, purpose, and self-determination.

lessons.title: Stages of change: where are you right now?
lessons.description: Introduces the Transtheoretical Model (precontemplation through maintenance) and helps participants honestly identify their current stage and what movement forward looks like.

lessons.title: Building a recovery identity: who am I becoming?
lessons.description: A reflective session focused on separating self-concept from substance use history and actively constructing a new, values-based identity in recovery.

lessons.title: Hope as a skill: how to cultivate it when it's hard
lessons.description: Examines hope not as a feeling but as a practice — drawing on SAMHSA's recovery principles to help participants build and sustain hope even during difficult periods.

lessons.title: What motivates your recovery? Finding your why
lessons.description: Helps participants identify intrinsic motivators and connect recovery goals to deeper values, relationships, and aspirations using motivational interviewing principles.

lessons.title: Understanding the disease model vs. recovery capital models
lessons.description: Compares biomedical and strengths-based frameworks for understanding addiction and recovery, helping participants find the model that resonates with their experience.

lessons.title: Early recovery survival guide: the first 90 days
lessons.description: Practical skills and expectations for navigating the most vulnerable period of recovery, including triggers, support systems, and daily structure.

lessons.title: The role of lived experience in peer support
lessons.description: Explores how shared experience creates connection and credibility in the peer support relationship, and how participants can begin to see their story as a resource.

lessons.title: Recovery is not linear: understanding setbacks without shame
lessons.description: Normalizes the non-linear nature of recovery and equips participants with frameworks for responding to setbacks without shame or hopelessness.

lessons.title: SAMHSA's four dimensions of recovery: a framework for your life
lessons.description: Uses SAMHSA's Health, Home, Purpose, and Community framework to help participants assess and plan for a balanced recovery life.

lessons.title: From surviving to thriving: what long-term recovery looks like
lessons.description: Inspires and informs participants about what sustained recovery can look like, featuring real-world examples and long-term recovery capital building.

lessons.title: The difference between abstinence and recovery
lessons.description: Distinguishes sobriety from full recovery, expanding the conversation to include mental health, relationships, purpose, and wellbeing.

lessons.title: Identifying your emotional triggers before they catch you off guard
lessons.description: Guided self-assessment to identify personal emotional triggers — situations, people, sensations — that increase vulnerability to use, with strategies for each.

lessons.title: Grounding techniques for moments of overwhelm
lessons.description: Teaches practical grounding techniques (5-4-3-2-1, box breathing, body scan) that participants can use in moments of acute stress, anxiety, or cravings.

lessons.title: The HALT check-in: recognizing when your body is sending signals
lessons.description: Introduces HALT (Hungry, Angry, Lonely, Tired) as a daily self-monitoring tool and builds habits for addressing each state before it becomes a crisis.

lessons.title: Healthy vs. unhealthy coping: learning the difference
lessons.description: Helps participants audit their current coping behaviors, distinguish adaptive from maladaptive strategies, and build a personal coping hierarchy.

lessons.title: Managing boredom in recovery without using
lessons.description: Addresses one of the most commonly underestimated relapse triggers, helping participants build meaningful activities and tolerate unstructured time.

lessons.title: Distress tolerance: riding out the wave
lessons.description: Based on DBT distress tolerance skills, teaches participants to survive emotional crises without making them worse, including urge surfing for cravings.

lessons.title: Anger in recovery: what it's telling you and what to do with it
lessons.description: Explores anger as information, teaches healthy expression and de-escalation strategies, and addresses how suppressed anger can increase relapse risk.

lessons.title: Anxiety management skills for everyday recovery
lessons.description: Practical anxiety management tools including cognitive restructuring, breathing techniques, and behavioral strategies appropriate for outpatient and mental health settings.

lessons.title: Building your personal coping toolkit
lessons.description: An individualized session where participants inventory their existing coping skills, identify gaps, and build a personalized written toolkit for future use.

lessons.title: Mindfulness basics: staying present without checking out
lessons.description: Introduces mindfulness as a recovery skill — not a spiritual practice — teaching participants simple awareness techniques for managing cravings and emotions.

lessons.title: The connection between emotions and cravings
lessons.description: Explores the emotional antecedents of cravings, helping participants recognize how feelings drive urges and build emotion-specific coping responses.

lessons.title: Emotional regulation when things feel out of control
lessons.description: Focuses on TIPP skills and other emotional regulation strategies for moments when feelings feel unmanageable, especially useful in mental health and dual diagnosis contexts.

lessons.title: Mapping your personal triggers: high-risk situations and warning signs
lessons.description: A structured individual session to identify internal and external triggers, map high-risk situations, and begin building a personalized risk awareness plan.

lessons.title: Building a written relapse prevention plan
lessons.description: Guides participants through creating a concrete, written relapse prevention plan including warning signs, supports, crisis contacts, and response steps.

lessons.title: Understanding cravings: why they happen and how long they last
lessons.description: Psychoeducation on the neuroscience of cravings — how they work, how long they typically last, and evidence-based strategies for managing them.

lessons.title: Managing cravings at social events and family gatherings
lessons.description: Scenario-based session on navigating high-exposure social situations: planning ahead, using supports, declining offers, and leaving gracefully if needed.

lessons.title: Recognizing the stages of relapse before the first use
lessons.description: Teaches the emotional, mental, and physical warning stages of relapse so participants can intervene early in the process, long before a return to use.

lessons.title: What to do after a setback: getting back on track without shame
lessons.description: A compassionate, action-oriented session addressing how to respond to a relapse or near-relapse — including reaching out for help, safety planning, and learning from the experience.

lessons.title: High-risk situations in early recovery: planning ahead
lessons.description: Identifies common high-risk scenarios in early recovery (celebrations, stress, conflict, anniversaries) and builds specific plans for navigating each.

lessons.title: The role of people, places, and things in relapse prevention
lessons.description: Examines environmental and social cues, helping participants make thoughtful decisions about relationships, locations, and objects associated with past use.

lessons.title: Overdose prevention and naloxone awareness
lessons.description: Provides education on overdose risk, recognition, and response, including naloxone access and how to create a safety plan with people in the participant's network.

lessons.title: Alcohol: understanding its unique risks in recovery
lessons.description: Addresses the often minimized risks of alcohol for people in recovery from other substances, including cross-addiction and the social normalization of drinking.

lessons.title: Creating a crisis response plan for cravings and urges
lessons.description: An individual session to build a personalized written plan for responding to acute cravings, including distraction, support contacts, and safe environments.

lessons.title: Harm reduction: meeting yourself where you are
lessons.description: Introduces harm reduction principles as a valid and evidence-based approach, helping participants reduce risk and stay connected to support regardless of where they are in their recovery.

lessons.title: Understanding depression in recovery: signs and strategies
lessons.description: Psychoeducation on depression as a common co-occurring condition in recovery, with evidence-based coping strategies and guidance on when to seek additional support.

lessons.title: Anxiety in recovery: more than just nerves
lessons.description: Distinguishes normal anxiety from anxiety disorders, teaches practical management tools, and addresses how anxiety and substance use interact in recovery.

lessons.title: Trauma awareness: what it is and how it shows up in recovery
lessons.description: Non-clinical psychoeducation on trauma — what it is, how it affects the nervous system and behavior, and trauma-informed strategies for daily living. Does not process trauma.

lessons.title: The basics of co-occurring disorders: when mental health and substance use overlap
lessons.description: Introductory psychoeducation on co-occurring disorders, helping participants understand the relationship between mental health conditions and substance use and why integrated support matters.

lessons.title: Sleep hygiene: why rest is a recovery skill
lessons.description: Addresses the significant impact of sleep on recovery, teaching practical sleep hygiene strategies and explaining how sleep deprivation increases relapse risk.

lessons.title: Medication adherence: why it matters and how to stay on track
lessons.description: Covers the importance of psychiatric and other medications in recovery, addresses stigma around medication, and builds practical systems for consistent adherence.

lessons.title: Self-care practices that actually work in recovery
lessons.description: Moves beyond generic self-care advice to build personalized, practical routines addressing physical, emotional, and social wellbeing in the context of recovery.

lessons.title: Grief and loss in recovery: it's not just about substances
lessons.description: A compassionate individual session exploring grief — including grief over the substance itself, lost relationships, and lost time — with healthy mourning strategies.

lessons.title: Understanding PTSD and its connection to substance use
lessons.description: Psychoeducation on PTSD symptoms and their relationship to substance use as self-medication, with emphasis on available supports and trauma-informed coping.

lessons.title: Managing stress without using: practical techniques
lessons.description: A practical toolkit session focused specifically on stress management strategies that don't involve substances, including time management, support-seeking, and relaxation techniques.

lessons.title: The mind-body connection: how your physical health affects your mental health
lessons.description: Explores bidirectional relationships between physical wellness and mental health in recovery, motivating participants to invest in physical self-care as a recovery strategy.

lessons.title: Recognizing when you need more support: knowing your limits
lessons.description: Helps participants develop self-awareness about when peer support is sufficient versus when to seek clinical, psychiatric, or crisis services — and how to take that step.

lessons.title: Setting healthy boundaries: what they are and how to hold them
lessons.description: Teaches the concept of boundaries as self-care tools, helps participants identify where they need boundaries, and builds specific, respectful language for setting them.

lessons.title: Rebuilding trust after addiction: a realistic roadmap
lessons.description: An honest individual session on the long-term work of rebuilding trust with family, partners, and friends — including managing expectations and handling disappointment.

lessons.title: Saying no without guilt: assertiveness in recovery
lessons.description: Teaches assertive communication skills with specific focus on declining offers of substances, resisting peer pressure, and saying no to requests that threaten recovery.

lessons.title: Healthy vs. unhealthy relationships: how to tell the difference
lessons.description: Uses a relationship quality framework to help participants evaluate their current relationships and make informed decisions about who to invest in during recovery.

lessons.title: Communication skills for difficult conversations
lessons.description: Practical training in communication strategies for high-stakes conversations: using I-statements, active listening, staying regulated, and de-escalating conflict.

lessons.title: Navigating romantic relationships in early recovery
lessons.description: Addresses the risks and realities of romantic relationships in early recovery, including timing, codependency patterns, and maintaining individual recovery work.

lessons.title: Family dynamics in recovery: understanding roles and patterns
lessons.description: Explores how family systems are shaped by addiction — including enabling, enmeshment, and communication breakdowns — and how recovery changes those dynamics.

lessons.title: Conflict resolution without escalation
lessons.description: Teaches a structured approach to resolving interpersonal conflict that de-escalates emotion, promotes understanding, and finds workable solutions.

lessons.title: How to make and keep sober friendships
lessons.description: Addresses the social isolation common in early recovery and teaches specific skills for initiating, developing, and maintaining sober friendships.

lessons.title: Co-dependency awareness: when helping becomes enabling
lessons.description: Introduces co-dependency concepts to help participants recognize unhealthy relational patterns — both in themselves and others — and build healthier dynamics.

lessons.title: Communicating your recovery needs to people who don't understand
lessons.description: Skills and scripts for explaining recovery needs to employers, family members, and others who may not understand addiction or peer support.

lessons.title: Forgiveness in recovery: what it means and what it doesn't
lessons.description: A nuanced exploration of forgiveness — of others and of oneself — that separates forgiveness from reconciliation and frames it as a personal healing tool.

lessons.title: Building a daily routine that supports your recovery
lessons.description: Helps participants design a structured daily routine — including sleep, meals, meetings, and meaningful activity — that reduces unstructured time and supports recovery.

lessons.title: Time management basics: getting things done without burning out
lessons.description: Practical time management techniques adapted for early recovery, including prioritization, scheduling, and managing fatigue and cognitive recovery effects.

lessons.title: Financial literacy 101: budgeting on a limited income
lessons.description: Introduces basic budgeting concepts — income tracking, expense categories, and savings habits — with specific attention to the financial realities of people in early recovery.

lessons.title: Nutrition basics for people in recovery
lessons.description: Addresses how substance use affects nutritional status and teaches basic healthy eating principles that support brain and body recovery.

lessons.title: Grocery shopping and meal prep on a budget
lessons.description: A practical skills session on food planning, smart shopping, and simple meal preparation that supports physical recovery on a limited budget.

lessons.title: Hygiene and self-care as acts of self-respect
lessons.description: Reframes personal hygiene and grooming as forms of self-respect and identity, building motivation and practical skills for consistent self-care routines.

lessons.title: Household management: keeping your space stable
lessons.description: Teaches basic household organization, cleaning routines, and home management skills, with emphasis on how a stable environment supports mental health and recovery.

lessons.title: Transportation planning: getting where you need to go
lessons.description: Practical session on navigating transportation options — public transit, ride sharing, community resources — for people in recovery who may lack reliable transportation.

lessons.title: Navigating technology: apps, accounts, and digital life
lessons.description: Covers basic digital literacy for recovery contexts: managing email and accounts, using recovery apps, and navigating government and healthcare portals online.

lessons.title: Managing medical appointments and healthcare systems
lessons.description: Teaches participants how to access healthcare, communicate with providers, manage medications, and navigate insurance or Medicaid — critical skills often disrupted by addiction.

lessons.title: Crisis planning: what to do when life goes sideways
lessons.description: Helps participants build a written personal crisis plan covering financial emergencies, housing loss, mental health crises, and relapse risk — with specific response steps.

lessons.title: How to ask for help: practicing the skill of reaching out
lessons.description: Addresses the stigma and vulnerability around asking for help, and teaches participants specific language and strategies for reaching out to supports before crisis hits.

lessons.title: Job readiness: are you ready to work and how do you know?
lessons.description: A candid self-assessment session on employment readiness — including stability, transportation, childcare, mental health, and the timing of returning to work in recovery.

lessons.title: Writing a resume with gaps in employment history
lessons.description: Practical resume-building session focused on how to address employment gaps honestly, highlight transferable skills, and frame recovery-era activities constructively.

lessons.title: Interview preparation: what to say (and not say) about your recovery
lessons.description: Scenario-based session on interview skills, including how to handle questions about employment gaps or criminal history without disclosing more than legally required.

lessons.title: Managing your recovery at work: staying balanced on the job
lessons.description: Strategies for maintaining recovery practices, managing stress, setting limits, and using EAPs or other supports in an employment setting.

lessons.title: Navigating workplace stress without using
lessons.description: Identifies common workplace stressors and builds a specific response plan for managing work-related stress without turning to substances.

lessons.title: Setting an education goal and making a plan to get there
lessons.description: Helps participants identify educational aspirations — GED, community college, vocational training — and build a concrete, realistic action plan with timelines.

lessons.title: Vocational training and trade programs as a path forward
lessons.description: Explores vocational and trade training as accessible, high-value alternatives to traditional college, including local resources and recovery-friendly programs.

lessons.title: Disclosing your recovery history to employers: yes, no, or when?
lessons.description: An honest exploration of disclosure decisions — legal rights, risks, and benefits — helping participants make informed, context-specific choices about sharing their story.

lessons.title: Handling rejection in the job search without it derailing your recovery
lessons.description: Addresses the emotional impact of rejection during job searching and builds specific coping and resilience strategies to stay motivated and recovery-focused.

lessons.title: Financial aid, grants, and scholarships for people in recovery
lessons.description: Provides an overview of funding opportunities for education and training, including recovery-specific scholarships, Pell grants, and state resources.

lessons.title: Workplace communication: professional relationships in early employment
lessons.description: Teaches professional communication norms for people re-entering the workforce, including email, punctuality, workplace conflict, and navigating authority relationships.

lessons.title: Volunteering as a bridge to employment
lessons.description: Explores volunteerism as a way to rebuild skills, address resume gaps, develop references, and re-engage with community while strengthening recovery.

lessons.title: Understanding your legal obligations: probation, parole, and court requirements
lessons.description: A practical overview of the conditions of supervised release, how to stay compliant, and what to do when facing violations or confusion about requirements.

lessons.title: Navigating the reentry process: what to expect in the first 30 days
lessons.description: Prepares participants for the immediate practical and emotional challenges of reentry — housing, ID, benefits, meetings, and support services in the critical first month.

lessons.title: Getting your ID and vital documents after incarceration
lessons.description: Step-by-step guidance on obtaining birth certificate, Social Security card, state ID, and other essential documents required to access housing, employment, and benefits.

lessons.title: Working with a probation or parole officer: building a productive relationship
lessons.description: Strategies for communicating clearly with supervision officers, meeting expectations consistently, and addressing problems proactively rather than reactively.

lessons.title: Restoring your rights: voting, housing, and employment after a felony
lessons.description: Educates participants on their rights post-conviction, including voting restoration, expungement opportunities, and anti-discrimination protections in employment and housing.

lessons.title: Understanding drug court and diversion programs
lessons.description: Explains drug court processes, expectations, and benefits — and how participants can make the most of diversion as a path toward recovery rather than punishment.

lessons.title: Housing after incarceration: what counts against you and how to find options
lessons.description: Practical guidance on navigating housing restrictions for people with criminal records — including what landlords can legally ask, fair chance housing, and transitional housing options.

lessons.title: Reentry planning before release: starting early
lessons.description: A pre-release session focused on building a reentry plan 60+ days before release, covering housing, employment, support networks, and recovery services.

lessons.title: Managing the stress of legal supervision without using
lessons.description: Addresses the unique stressors of supervised release — check-ins, restrictions, fear of violations — and builds specific coping strategies for managing them in recovery.

lessons.title: Navigating the court system: understanding your case and your rights
lessons.description: Demystifies the criminal court process and helps participants understand their rights, the role of their attorney, and how to participate meaningfully in their case.

lessons.title: From institutional thinking to community living: making the mindset shift
lessons.description: Addresses the cognitive and behavioral adjustments required when transitioning from a correctional setting to the community, including decision-making, authority, and self-direction.

lessons.title: Building your recovery support network from scratch
lessons.description: A structured session on intentionally identifying and cultivating a recovery support network — including peers, sponsors, family, professionals, and community resources.

lessons.title: Finding and attending your first recovery meeting
lessons.description: Practical preparation for attending 12-Step, SMART Recovery, or other peer support meetings for the first time, including what to expect, how to introduce yourself, and finding the right fit.

lessons.title: Volunteering as a recovery tool: giving back builds community
lessons.description: Explores the research-backed recovery benefits of giving back, and helps participants identify volunteer opportunities that match their interests and schedule.

lessons.title: Navigating sober socializing: parties, holidays, and events
lessons.description: Scenario-based session on attending social events without using — planning ahead, using exit strategies, managing social pressure, and building confidence.

lessons.title: Loneliness in recovery: why it's common and what to do about it
lessons.description: Validates the isolation that often accompanies early recovery and teaches specific, actionable strategies for building connection and combating chronic loneliness.

lessons.title: What peer support relationships look like and why they work
lessons.description: Educates participants on the unique nature of peer support — how it differs from clinical care, why lived experience matters, and how to make the most of peer relationships.

lessons.title: Getting involved in your recovery community
lessons.description: Introduces participants to recovery community organizations, advocacy opportunities, and peer-led events that offer belonging, purpose, and meaningful connection.

lessons.title: Social media and recovery: when connection becomes comparison
lessons.description: Addresses the double-edged nature of social media in recovery — its value for connection and community versus its risks for comparison, envy, and exposure to using content.

lessons.title: Navigating neighborhoods with high exposure to substances
lessons.description: Practical strategies for managing recovery in environments with high drug and alcohol availability — including route planning, time management, and building safer routines.

lessons.title: Faith communities and recovery: finding your fit
lessons.description: Explores the role of faith and spirituality in recovery across diverse traditions, and helps participants navigate faith communities in a way that supports their recovery.

lessons.title: Creating new rituals and traditions that don't involve using
lessons.description: Helps participants intentionally build new celebrations, routines, and rituals that provide meaning and connection without substances.

lessons.title: Giving back: becoming a peer supporter yourself
lessons.description: Explores the pathway to peer support certification, the recovery benefits of helping others, and how participants can begin contributing their lived experience.

lessons.title: Exploring your values: what matters most to you in recovery?
lessons.description: A structured values clarification exercise helping participants identify their core values and begin aligning daily decisions with what they believe in most.

lessons.title: Recovery identity: who you are beyond your history of use
lessons.description: Focuses on separating personal identity from addiction history, building a narrative of self that encompasses growth, resilience, and possibility.

lessons.title: Setting meaningful goals that align with your values
lessons.description: Connects values work to SMART goal-setting, helping participants create goals that feel personally meaningful rather than externally imposed.

lessons.title: Shame and stigma in recovery: understanding and releasing them
lessons.description: Examines both internal shame and external stigma, offering frameworks for releasing shame that don't require public disclosure while building self-compassion.

lessons.title: Spirituality in recovery: a non-denominational exploration
lessons.description: An inclusive exploration of spirituality as a recovery resource — meaning, connection, transcendence — that doesn't require religious affiliation or belief.

lessons.title: Celebrating recovery milestones: why it matters
lessons.description: Makes the case for intentional milestone recognition in recovery and helps the group create meaningful ways to acknowledge and celebrate progress.

lessons.title: Self-esteem in recovery: rebuilding a positive self-image
lessons.description: Addresses the erosion of self-esteem common in addiction and recovery, teaching cognitive and behavioral strategies for rebuilding a positive, realistic self-image.

lessons.title: Finding meaning and purpose beyond sobriety
lessons.description: Helps participants explore vocation, contribution, creativity, and connection as sources of purpose that give recovery a reason beyond abstinence.

lessons.title: The power of storytelling: using your lived experience
lessons.description: Explores how participants can begin to share their recovery story as a tool for connection, advocacy, and helping others — with guidance on healthy boundaries in sharing.

lessons.title: Accepting yourself in the present while working toward the future
lessons.description: A session on radical acceptance and self-compassion — holding the tension between being a work in progress and being worthy of dignity and love right now.

lessons.title: Recovery as a second chance: reimagining your life narrative
lessons.description: A strength-based session on reauthoring the personal narrative — seeing recovery not as what was lost but as a foundation for what is possible.

lessons.title: Exploring creativity and self-expression in recovery
lessons.description: Introduces creative outlets (writing, art, music, movement) as recovery tools for processing emotion, building identity, and finding joy.

lessons.title: Exercise in recovery: getting started without overdoing it
lessons.description: Introduces physical activity as a recovery-supporting practice, with accessible starting points, the neuroscience of exercise in recovery, and guidance on avoiding compulsive patterns.

lessons.title: Nutrition and the recovering brain: what your body needs
lessons.description: Covers the nutritional needs specific to recovery — blood sugar regulation, neurotransmitter precursors, hydration — and practical dietary changes participants can make.

lessons.title: What substances do to the body: understanding the physical toll
lessons.description: Non-judgmental psychoeducation on the physical effects of various substances on the brain, liver, cardiovascular system, and other organs, framed as motivation for healing.

lessons.title: Managing chronic pain without substances
lessons.description: Addresses the challenge of living with chronic pain in recovery, exploring non-opioid pain management strategies including movement, mindfulness, physical therapy, and advocacy with providers.

lessons.title: Preventive healthcare in recovery: getting back to the doctor
lessons.description: Overcomes barriers to healthcare access in recovery — including stigma, insurance navigation, and healthcare avoidance — and motivates participants to prioritize preventive care.

lessons.title: Sexual health in recovery: a healthy conversation
lessons.description: Addresses sexual health topics relevant to recovery including consent, safe sex, intimacy in recovery, and the effects of substances on sexual health and decision-making.

lessons.title: Tobacco and recovery: the often overlooked addiction
lessons.description: Examines tobacco use in the recovery population, its health consequences, and evidence-based cessation strategies in a non-shaming, motivating format.

lessons.title: Caffeine, sugar, and other substitute behaviors in early recovery
lessons.description: Addresses cross-addiction and substitute behaviors common in early recovery — including caffeine, sugar, and screen time — with a harm-reduction and awareness lens.

lessons.title: Healing the gut: how substances affect digestion and what helps
lessons.description: Covers the GI effects of substance use and early recovery, including nausea, appetite disruption, and practical strategies for digestive health during recovery.

lessons.title: Body image in recovery: making peace with your physical self
lessons.description: Addresses body image concerns common in recovery — including weight changes, physical damage, and appearance-related shame — with self-compassion and body-neutral strategies.

lessons.title: Understanding hepatitis C and liver health for people in recovery
lessons.description: Targeted health education on hepatitis C risk, testing, treatment, and liver health for populations at higher risk due to injection drug use or alcohol use.

lessons.title: The connection between physical health and mental health in recovery
lessons.description: Explores bidirectional relationships between physical wellness practices and mental health outcomes in recovery, motivating integrated self-care.

lessons.title: Preparing for release: reentry planning 60 days before you leave
lessons.description: A structured pre-release planning session covering housing, employment, benefits, transportation, and recovery support services — designed to reduce chaos in the first days after release.

lessons.title: Transitioning from incarceration to community: mindset and expectations
lessons.description: Addresses the psychological and behavioral adjustment required when moving from a controlled institutional environment to independent community living.

lessons.title: Peer pressure in adolescence: when your friends use and you don't
lessons.description: Age-appropriate session helping young people identify and respond to peer pressure around substances, with practical refusal scripts and social alternatives.

lessons.title: Social media, comparison, and recovery for young people
lessons.description: Tailored for adolescents and young adults, examining how social media affects self-image, peer norms around substances, and recovery motivation.

lessons.title: School reintegration after treatment: navigating academic life in recovery
lessons.description: Practical guidance for young people returning to school after treatment, including managing relationships, academic catch-up, and disclosure decisions.

lessons.title: Family communication for teens in recovery
lessons.description: Skills and strategies for young people to communicate their needs, set limits, and navigate family conflict while maintaining their recovery.

lessons.title: Motherhood and recovery: navigating parenting while healing
lessons.description: Supports women in recovery who are parenting, addressing guilt, practical parenting challenges, child welfare system navigation, and building parenting confidence.

lessons.title: Domestic violence awareness: recognizing patterns and finding safety
lessons.description: Provides education on domestic violence warning signs, safety planning, and resources — framed as empowerment and information rather than crisis intervention.

lessons.title: Women's health in recovery: topics specific to women's experience
lessons.description: Covers reproductive health, hormonal impacts of substances, sexual trauma awareness, and women-specific recovery considerations in a safe, women-centered format.

lessons.title: Understanding co-occurring disorders: when the diagnosis is more than one thing
lessons.description: Psychoeducation for dual diagnosis populations on how mental health conditions and substance use disorders interact, reinforce each other, and require integrated support.

lessons.title: Medication management for co-occurring mental health and substance use
lessons.description: Practical guidance for people managing psychiatric medications alongside substance use recovery, including adherence, side effects, and communication with prescribers.

lessons.title: When mental health and substance use feed each other: breaking the cycle
lessons.description: An advanced dual diagnosis session exploring the self-medication hypothesis and practical tools for interrupting the cycle between mental health symptoms and substance use.

lessons.title: Occupational identity and recovery for veterans and first responders
lessons.description: Explores how professional identity, culture, and occupational trauma affect recovery for veterans and first responders, with strengths-based reintegration strategies.

lessons.title: Transitioning from military to civilian life: a recovery lens
lessons.description: Addresses the unique challenges veterans face in transitioning to civilian life — including identity shifts, culture shock, and finding new purpose — within a recovery framework.
